audience: Start-ups, self-employed professionals and SMEs planning a new website and unsure about the right scope
primaryDutchSearchIntent: KMO's helpen kiezen tussen een onepager en een volledige website op basis van hun aanbod, doelgroep, vindbaarheid en groeiplannen

searchIntent.question: Should an SME choose a one-page website or a full multi-page website?
searchIntent.geographicContext: Belgium and the wider English-speaking market

directAnswer: Choose a one-page website when you have one compact offer, a largely uniform audience and one main conversion. Choose a full website when services, audiences or search intents need separate explanations, evidence and conversion paths, or when the business expects substantial content and growth.
title: One-page or full website: which suits your SME?
metaDescription: Choosing a one-page or full website? Compare SEO, costs, content, conversion and scalability to find the right structure for your SME.
h1: One-page or full website: which suits your SME?

internalLinks.anchor: complete guide to commissioning a website
how website speed and SEO work together

factsToPreserve: The choice is not a contest between cheap and professional; either format can be well executed
A one-page website is suitable for one compact offer, a largely uniform audience and one primary conversion
A full website is better when services, audiences, search intents or functionality need distinct routes
More pages are valuable only when each page has a unique purpose
Google does not identify page type or page count as a ranking criterion in its own right in the cited guidance
One URL has one title, description and link destination, which limits how precisely it can serve distinct intents
Anchor links remain part of the same URL and do not replace standalone landing pages
A one-page website often has a smaller scope but is not automatically simple, fast to produce or cheap
Accessibility, analytics, forms, media and testing matter for either format
A one-page website can evolve into a full website when expansion is anticipated in content and technology
Core Web Vitals matter, but format alone does not determine performance and good performance is not the only ranking factor
The recommended structure is the smallest one that can carry the complete story and expected growth
